title: Attack Mechanics and Combat Actions
description: Rules for making attacks, opportunity attacks, and combat-specific actions

making_attacks.description: When you take the Attack action, you make an attack. Some other actions, Bonus Actions, and Reactions also let you make an attack
making_attacks.attack_types: Whether you strike with a Melee weapon, fire a Ranged weapon, or make an attack roll as part of a spell, an attack has the following structure

attack_structure.title: Choose a Target
attack_structure.description: Pick a target within your attack's range: a creature, an object, or a location

attack_structure.title: Determine Modifiers
attack_structure.description: The DM determines whether the target has Cover and whether you have Advantage or Disadvantage against the target. In addition, spells, special abilities, and other effects can apply penalties or bonuses to your attack roll

attack_structure.title: Resolve the Attack
attack_structure.description: Make the attack roll, as detailed earlier in this chapter. On a hit, you roll damage unless the particular attack has rules that specify otherwise. Some attacks cause special effects in addition to or instead of damage

cover.description: Walls, trees, creatures, and other obstacles can provide cover, making a target more difficult to harm
cover.benefit_requirement: A target can benefit from cover only when an attack or other effect originates on the opposite side of the cover
cover.multiple_cover: If a target is behind multiple sources of cover, only the most protective degree of cover applies; the degrees aren't added together. For example, if a target is behind a creature that gives Half Cover and a tree trunk that gives Three-Quarters Cover, the target has Three-Quarters Cover

cover_types.benefit: +2 bonus to AC and Dexterity saving throws
cover_types.offered_by: Another creature or an object that covers at least half of the target

cover_types.benefit: +5 bonus to AC and Dexterity saving throws
cover_types.offered_by: An object that covers at least three-quarters of the target
An object that covers the whole target

attacking_unseen_targets.disadvantage: When you make an attack roll against a target you can't see, you have Disadvantage on the roll
attacking_unseen_targets.application: This is true whether you're guessing the target's location or targeting a creature you can hear but not see
attacking_unseen_targets.missing: If the target isn't in the location you targeted, you miss

being_unseen.advantage: When a creature can't see you, you have Advantage on attack rolls against it
being_unseen.location_reveal: If you are hidden when you make an attack roll, you give away your location when the attack hits or misses

ranged_attacks.description: When you make a ranged attack, you fire a bow, hurl an axe, or otherwise send projectiles to strike a foe at a distance. Many spells also involve making a ranged attack

range.single_range: If a ranged attack, such as one made with a spell, has a single range, you can't attack a target beyond this range
range.dual_range: Some ranged attacks, such as those made with a Longbow, have two ranges. The smaller number is the normal range, and the larger number is the long range
range.range_penalties: Your attack roll has Disadvantage when your target is beyond normal range, and you can't attack a target beyond long range

close_combat.description: Aiming a ranged attack is more difficult when a foe is next to you
close_combat.disadvantage: When you make a ranged attack roll with a weapon, a spell, or some other means, you have Disadvantage on the roll if you are within 5 feet of an enemy who can see you and doesn't have the Incapacitated condition

melee_attacks.description: A melee attack allows you to attack a target within your reach. A melee attack typically uses a handheld weapon or an Unarmed Strike. Many monsters make melee attacks with claws, teeth, or other body parts. A few spells also involve melee attacks

reach.standard: A creature has a 5-foot reach and can thus attack targets within 5 feet when making a melee attack
reach.extended: Certain creatures have melee attacks with a reach greater than 5 feet, as noted in their descriptions

opportunity_attacks.description: Combatants watch for enemies to drop their guard. If you move heedlessly past your foes, you put yourself in danger by provoking an Opportunity Attack

avoiding.disengage: You can avoid provoking an Opportunity Attack by taking the Disengage action
avoiding.exceptions: You also don't provoke an Opportunity Attack when you teleport or when you are moved without using your movement, action, Bonus Action, or Reaction
avoiding.examples: For example, you don't provoke an Opportunity Attack if an explosion hurls you out of a foe's reach or if you fall past an enemy

making.trigger: You can make an Opportunity Attack when a creature that you can see leaves your reach
making.reaction: To make the attack, take a Reaction to make one melee attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike against that creature
making.timing: The attack occurs right before it leaves your reach

ability_modifiers.strength: Used for melee weapon attacks and thrown weapon attacks
ability_modifiers.dexterity: Used for ranged weapon attacks and finesse weapon attacks
ability_modifiers.spell_attacks: Use spellcasting ability modifier as determined by class

attack_modifiers.proficiency_bonus: Add to attack rolls when proficient with the weapon or when making spell attacks
